Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)

Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
William Bellor-Yeh, Esq. (209.227.3770; william@belloryehlawoffice.com) Specially appearing for Plaintiff.
Stephen Jones (415.265.7204; stephen_jones87@yahoo.com) in propria persona as Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THE AGILIS GROUP LLC'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment

Defendant Agilis Group, LLC's motion to set aside default is denied.

"When service of a summons has not resulted in actual notice to a party in time to defend the action and a default or default judgment has been entered against him or her in the action, he or she may serve and file a notice of motion to set aside the default or default judgment and for leave to defend the action." (Code of Civ. Proc. section 473.5.) Here, the proof of service declares that on October 28, 2019, the registered agent for service of process, Mr. Stephen Jones, was personally served with the summons and complaint. (Kerfan Decl. Exhibit 1.) Defendant has failed to show "that he has a good defense to the action on the merits which he could have presented" prior to the entry of the judgment. (See Thompson v. Sutton, supra, at p. 276.) Further, under California Code of Civil Procedure section 1788.61, Defendant has failed to satisfy the statutory filing requirement by not including a copy of the proposed pleading with the motion for relief. (See Cal. Civ. Code section 1788.61(b).) Accordingly, the motion must be denied.

Regardless, a corporation cannot represent itself in court, either in propria persona or through an officer or agent who is not an attorney. (Paradise v. Nowlin (1948) 86 Cal.App.2d 897, 195; Himmel v. City Council (1959) 169 Cal.App.2d 97, 336.)" "A corporation is an artificial legal entity; it cannot represent itself in court in propria persona but must appear through an agent who is a member of the bar. (See Merco Constr. Engineers, Inc. v. Municipal Court (1978) 21 Cal.3d 724, 729-730, 147.) Here, Mr. Stephen Jones attempted to file these motions in pro per. Under the circumstances, the motion to set aside default is "void by reason of the corporation's lack of power to represent itself in an action in court" (Paradise v. Nowlin (1948) 86 Cal.App.2d 897, 898).

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)
